title: Avoid Catching Throwable and Error in Java
description: Throwable is the superclass of all errors and exceptions in Java, and Error is the superclass of all errors. Catching either Throwable or Error will also catch severe issues like OutOfMemoryError and InternalError, from which an application should not attempt to recover. Instead, catch specific exceptions like RuntimeException or custom exceptions.
recommendation: Replace the catch block of Throwable or Error with RuntimeException or your custom exception.
impact: Catching Throwable or Error can lead to catching severe system errors from which recovery may not be possible, leading to unexpected application behavior.

checks.message: Replace the catch block of Throwable or Error with RuntimeException or your custom exception.
